summary: Privacy section in Settings tab fully tested end-to-end (backend + frontend mobile UI). All 5 new backend tests pass and all UI flows (modal, persistence, navigation, back) work as expected.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py has grown to ~560 lines and is approaching the 700-line guideline. Consider splitting into routers (auth, users, contacts, alerts) in a future iteration.
Non-blocking deprecation warnings in console: 'shadow*' style props deprecated (use boxShadow), props.pointerEvents deprecated, useNativeDriver fallback on web. None affect functionality.
seed_data_creation: Used existing alice@test.com seed user. Test resets who_can_add back to 'everyone' at the end for clean state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Privacy feature is stable. Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_privacy.py. Login testIDs: login-email-input, login-password-input, login-submit-button. Settings testIDs: settings-screen, settings-who-can-add, settings-blocked-contacts, who-can-add-sheet, who-opt-everyone|contacts|nobody, blocked-screen, blocked-back. EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL is the env var (not EXPO_BACKEND_URL).
rca_of_issue: N/A - feature works as designed. Verified: (1) GET /api/users/me/blocked returns [] for Alice (status 200, requires auth - returns 401 without). (2) PATCH /api/users/me/settings with who_can_add=nobody/contacts/everyone persists; /api/auth/me reflects updated value. (3) UI: 'Who can add me' row opens modal with 3 options, tapping 'My Contacts Only' closes modal and updates row label, value persists across page reload. (4) 'Blocked contacts' row navigates to /blocked screen showing 'You haven't blocked anyone' empty state with ban icon. (5) Back button returns to Settings.
